seoTitle: How to Generate API Keys for Brevo
metaDescription: Create and secure API credentials in Brevo for connecting external tools, forms, or CRMs, with custom expiration rules for each integration key.
intro: Connecting external forms, CRM platforms, or automated syncs to Brevo shouldn't mean exposing your main account password. Brevo's administration panel lets you generate isolated API keys with custom expiration rules, keeping each integration's access scoped and revocable independently.

steps.heading: Locate the API key center
steps.body: Open your main profile drop-down menu and navigate to the protected Senders and API tabs where key management lives.

steps.heading: Start a new key
steps.body: Choose to create a new API key from the developer backend rather than reusing an existing one for a new integration.

steps.heading: Name your integration
steps.body: Give the key a clear tracking profile name so you can identify which application or service it belongs to later.

steps.heading: Set an expiration rule
steps.body: Assign a specific expiration timeline, ranging from a short 7-day window to a permanent lifetime key, depending on the integration's needs.

steps.heading: Store the key securely
steps.body: Copy the generated character string immediately and store it in a secure location, since production keys should never be shared or hardcoded in public files.

faq.question: Why shouldn't I share my Brevo account password for integrations?
faq.answer: API keys isolate access per application, so if one integration is compromised you can revoke that specific key without affecting your entire account.

faq.question: Can I set an API key to expire automatically?
faq.answer: Yes, you can assign expiration timelines ranging from 7 days to a permanent lifetime key.

faq.question: What if I lose my API key after generating it?
faq.answer: You typically can't view the full key again after creation, so you'll need to generate a new one and update your integration.

faq.question: Does Brevo support integrations for AI tools?
faq.answer: Yes, Brevo's newer security infrastructure includes native Model Context Protocol options aimed at AI integrations.
